mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a positive return differential of at least 0.5% over 60 days in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above the 10-month moving average indicates accelerating supply-side capacity utilization that supports short-term equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle phases or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge (t-stat < 1.5) in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity that improves corporate revenue outlooks and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions correlate with negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers inflation fears that force central banks to tighten policy expectations, producing negative equity returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Elevated inflation prints fail to predict negative returns or the mechanism yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining housing starts and rising retail sales indicates a sectoral rotation away from durable goods that weakens broad market earnings and drives negative 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence signal produces positive returns in 65% of cases or loses predictive power when housing inventory is low.

mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates during a monetary easing cycle compress equity discount rates and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rate cuts during easing cycles fail to generate positive 5-day returns or the signal becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.
